TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON JUL-15-2010 IN 206, DEFENDANT THE DONALD LEVINE REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT. ROBERT DE VRIES APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JEFFREY GOODRICH APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT THE DONALD LEVINE REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST. THE COURT CORRECTED MR. DEVRIES' UNDERSTANDING OF THE ORDER. THE COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO ORDER AN UNNAMED BROKER TO INCLUDE, ACCEPT, ADHERE TO A TERM REQUIRING THE BROKER TO SEND ALL COMMUNICATIONS BETWEEN THE PRINCIPAL AND THE BROKER TO MR. DEVRIES' CLIENT. INSTEAD, THE PROPER UNDERSTANDING OF THE ORDER AND THE INTENT OF THE COURT WAS THAT ANY COMMUNICATIONS BETWEEN THE PRINCIPAL AND THE BROKER WILL BE SENT TO MR. DEVRIES, LAWYER TO LAWYER, AS A RESPONSIBILITY OF THE PRINCIPAL. THE COURT MADE CLEAR WITH MR. DEVRIES THAT HE WITHDREW HIS OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION BECAUSE THE UNDERSTANDING OF THE ORDER HAD BEEN CLARIFIED BY THE COURT. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT THERE HAS BEEN, AT LEAST FROM PLAINTIFF'S PAPERS, AN EFFORT TO INTERFERE WITH THE EXECUTION OF THE COURT'S ORDER. THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE NO LATER THAN MONDAY OR AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. IF DEFENDANT HAS ANY INTEREST IN ADDING ANYTHING TO THE COURT'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE, DEFENDANT SHALL COMMUNICATE BY MEANS OF A LETTER OR A DECLARATION, WHICH IS PREFERABLE, COPIED TO MR. DEVRIES AS TO ANY OTHER INSTANCES THAT DEFENDANT FEELS WOULD CONSTITUTE AN ACT OF CONTEMPT. MR. DEVRIES WITHDREW HIS OPPOSITION TO THE ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. JUDGMENT SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)
